title: astragalus denudatus
intro: Astragalus denudatus, also known as milk vetch or white milk vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to North America, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and white or pinkish pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting effects. Astragalus denudatus is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fatigue, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, as well as potentially reducing stress and anxiety. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its medicinal properties.
